mainEntity.name: Why does the MdWrk lander compile static HTML?

acceptedAnswer.text: Static HTML makes the primary public content readable before JavaScript, which supports search, accessibility, answer engines, and deployment-independent verification.

mainEntity.name: What is the MdWrk extension platform?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is the governed package and runtime surface used to describe how extensions integrate with the Markdown workspace.

mainEntity.name: Are extensions automatically trusted?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. The documentation should describe manifest metadata, compatibility checks, and explicit trust boundaries instead of implying automatic trust.

@graph.name: Local-first Markdown workspace for portable documents — MdWrk
@graph.headline: Local-first Markdown workspace
@graph.description: MdWrk documents a local-first Markdown workflow where writing, preview, workspace state, extension behavior, and export boundaries are explicit.

mainEntity.name: What does local-first mean for MdWrk?

acceptedAnswer.text: Local-first means the product documentation treats local writing and preview as the default mental model, while sync, export, and external integrations are opt-in workflows.

@graph.name: Offline Markdown editor for local-first writing — MdWrk
@graph.headline: Offline Markdown editor for local-first writing
@graph.description: MdWrk is an offline-capable Markdown editor for writing, previewing, organizing, and extending Markdown documents while preserving local workflow control.

mainEntity.name: Is MdWrk an offline Markdown editor?

acceptedAnswer.text: MdWrk is designed around offline-capable authoring. Public docs distinguish local writing and preview from workflows that intentionally contact external services.

mainEntity.name: Does MdWrk require cloud storage?

acceptedAnswer.text: MdWrk does not make hosted document storage the default. Sync and export are documented as deliberate workflow choices.
